input.headline: Fiber is something most people could use more of. But experts advise caution with ‘fibermaxxing’

raw.headline: Fiber is something most people could use more of. But experts advise caution with ‘fibermaxxing’

winner: Why did the guy who ate too much fiber at once call an ambulance? Because he thought his body was trying to *fiber* him—like "fiber optics," but way more confusing!

candidates.text: I told my doctor I was trying to "fibermax" myself—he said that's why I've been seeing more wood lately!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'fibermaxxing' and 'seeing more wood' are clever and specific, with a natural flow and surprising twist."}

candidates.text: I tried fibermaxxing last night and now I’m just a human prune. The doctor said I need to slow down—turns out my digestive system can’t handle being a natural laxative.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific analogy with humor, concise and natural language."}

candidates.text: I tried to go all-out on fiber—ate a whole wheat pizza and followed up with bran muffins for dessert—but my colon said, "We don’t need this much roughage, buddy.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Funny, specific, and uses a relatable scenario with a clever twist involving the colon."}

candidates.text: Why did the guy who ate too much fiber at once call an ambulance? Because he thought his body was trying to *fiber* him—like "fiber optics," but way more confusing!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever with the play on 'fiber' and 'fiber optics,' and it's concise. However, the humor could be more surprising and the punchline feels slightly forced."}

candidates.text: My doctor says I need to eat more fiber, so I’ve started eating more fiber. It’s a rougher texture than I’m used to, but I suppose that’s the point—you’ve got to get out of your comfort zone if you want to see real progress.
